title: BMI Calculator
description: Calculate your BMI from height and weight. Get your weight category and see how far you are from a healthy range.
hint: Enter your height and weight to calculate your BMI.
disclaimer: * BMI is a general indicator of body size. It is not a substitute for medical advice.

results.needGain: You are {kg} kg below a normal weight (BMI 22)
results.needLoss: Losing {kg} kg would bring you to a normal weight (BMI 22)
results.idealNote: Based on WHO reference BMI of 22

howToUse.label: Choose your unit system
howToUse.description: Select Metric (cm / kg) or Imperial (ft / lbs) depending on the units you prefer.

howToUse.label: Enter your height
howToUse.description: In metric mode, type your height in centimetres. In imperial mode, enter feet and inches separately.

howToUse.label: Enter your weight
howToUse.description: Type your weight in kilograms (metric) or pounds (imperial).

howToUse.label: Read your results
howToUse.description: Your BMI value and weight category appear instantly. You can also see how much you would need to gain or lose to reach a BMI of 22, the standard reference weight.

faq.question: What is BMI?
faq.answer: BMI (Body Mass Index) is calculated by dividing your weight in kilograms by the square of your height in metres. It is an internationally recognised indicator of body size. A BMI below 18.5 is underweight, 18.5–24.9 is normal, 25–29.9 is overweight, and 30 or above is obese (WHO classification).

faq.question: Can BMI alone determine my health?
faq.answer: BMI is a useful screening tool but does not account for muscle mass, bone density, or fat distribution. Athletes and older adults in particular may get misleading results. Please consult a doctor or health professional for a complete assessment.

faq.question: Why is BMI 22 used as the ideal weight reference?
faq.answer: BMI 22 is associated with the lowest disease risk according to several epidemiological studies and is widely used as a reference point for calculating an ideal body weight.

faq.question: Is my data sent to a server?
faq.answer: No. All calculations are performed entirely in your browser. Your height, weight, and any other information you enter are never sent to or stored on any server.
